strengths: Strong debugging skills — tracked down a LayerNorm inverse transform bug that recovered 47.5pp of model accuracy without retraining
Can work across the full stack — built everything from PyTorch model architectures to React frontends to REST APIs
Willing to iterate aggressively — went through 10 architecture iterations on the Minecraft voxel generation project to improve accuracy from 21.6% to 66.9%
weaknesses: Fill in your real weaknesses here — be honest, the agent will challenge you on these
Example: Sometimes go too deep into implementation before stepping back to validate the approach

stories.title: Debugging the VAE generation pipeline
stories.situation: The Minecraft 3D voxel generation model was producing garbled outputs despite the VAE training looking healthy. Reconstruction accuracy was stuck at ~19%.
stories.task: Find the root cause of generation failures and fix the pipeline without retraining the model from scratch.
stories.action: Traced the issue to a Straight-Through Estimator implementation problem and a LayerNorm inverse transform bug in the generation pipeline. Implemented the STE fix and corrected the LayerNorm transform.
stories.result: Recovered 47.5 percentage points of accuracy without any model retraining. Final reconstruction accuracy reached 66.9% after architecture iteration.
stories.lessons: Always validate the full inference pipeline separately from training. A model can train perfectly but still fail if the generation path has bugs.

stories.title: Building the RAG chatbot at Driving Forward
stories.situation: Driving Forward needed a way for students to get answers about 40+ university partner programs, but the information was scattered across different websites with no unified search.
stories.task: Build a chatbot that could accurately answer questions using real university data while minimizing hallucinations.
stories.action: Built a RAG pipeline using Google Gemini with ChromaDB for vector storage. Ingested and indexed data from 40+ university websites using semantic embeddings. Built the full stack — React frontend, FastAPI backend with REST APIs.
stories.result: Reduced LLM hallucinations by 60%. Deployed on Render with <80ms average response latency.
stories.lessons: Fill in what you learned from this experience.

stories.title: Designing the multi-agent debate system
stories.situation: Wanted to explore whether multiple AI agents debating could produce more reliable research analysis than a single LLM.
stories.task: Design and build a multi-agent system that could conduct structured research debates with real academic sources.
stories.action: Architected 11 Claude Code agents across three epistemological roles. Built a supervisor that orchestrates 2-round debates with post-debate falsification and overconfidence calibration. Integrated arXiv and Semantic Scholar APIs for evidence retrieval.
stories.result: System aggregates findings from 15+ academic surveys per query. Caching reduces repeat query latency to <100ms.
stories.lessons: Fill in what you learned about multi-agent systems, LLM limitations, etc.

stories.title: Rainier Route — solving a personal commute problem
stories.situation: Seattle's public transit and biking infrastructure are disconnected — no app combines bike routes with transit schedules for multi-modal commuting.
stories.task: Build an app that generates optimal bike-to-transit route combinations for Seattle commuters.
stories.action: Designed a multi-modal routing engine scoring 5 parallel route combinations using Haversine distance optimization. Built a Node.js API orchestrating Google Maps and GTFS-Realtime feeds. Built React frontend with dynamic polyline rendering.
stories.result: Reduced estimated commute times by up to 20%. Under 500ms per route calculation. 4.3/5 usability rating in user testing.
stories.lessons: Fill in what you learned from this project.

stories.title: Syncy — wrangling 4 different OAuth flows
stories.situation: Wanted to build a playlist sharing app but each music streaming service (Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, SpotAPI) has completely different authentication patterns — OAuth 2.0 PKCE, MusicKit JS, device code flows.
stories.task: Build a unified auth and track matching system across 4 platforms with different APIs, token formats, and music catalogs.
stories.action: Implemented OAuth 2.0 PKCE and device code flows with encrypted token storage and auto-refresh. Engineered a track normalization pipeline using ISRC codes for cross-platform matching with a fuzzy search fallback (artist + title + duration tolerance). Built a TrackMapping cache layer to minimize redundant API calls.
stories.result: Platform supports playlist conversion across all 4 services with batch processing, duplicate prevention, and JSONB track metadata storage across 5 database models.
stories.lessons: Fill in what you learned — dealing with inconsistent APIs, caching strategies, etc.

stories.title: Emergency Copilot — real-time AI threat detection hackathon
stories.situation: Hackathon project to build an AI-powered CCTV surveillance platform that could detect threats in real-time and alert dispatchers.
stories.task: Design and build a two-stage detection pipeline and real-time dispatcher dashboard under time pressure.
stories.action: Built a two-stage pipeline: Overshoot SDK for rapid 1-second clip analysis, then batched snapshots fed into Gemini 2.5-flash for detailed incident timelines. Engineered WebSocket snapshot ingestion with SSE broadcast, Haversine-based geospatial clustering (50m radius, 1hr window), and LiveKit WebRTC for sub-second latency video streaming. Built dispatcher dashboard with Leaflet map, live feeds, and AI timelines.
stories.result: Full working platform with 4 database tables, 7 custom React hooks, real-time alert system, and AI-generated event timelines with confidence scoring.
stories.lessons: Fill in what you learned — working under pressure, making architecture decisions fast, team coordination, etc.

values: Fill in what drives you — why CS/DS, what problems excite you
Example: I care about building things that solve real problems I've personally experienced (like Rainier Route)
